Clicking the charcoal box did nothing either. Let me think about this more carefully.

3. Red crosses → nothing

The only interactive elements are the N and Y shapes inside the containers. But I've tried all 4 combinations of seed states without completing the level.
Wait, maybe I need to look at this from a different angle. What if the goal is not about the seeds at all, but about the relationship between the container contents?
Looking at the containers:
- Top container: two N (light green) shapes
- Bottom container: two Y (yellow) shapes
What if clicking on an N shape changes it to Y, or vice versa? Let me check the history to see if the N/Y shapes inside containers have ever changed color.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the initial state from history
print(f"History length: {len(history)}")
print(f"Transitions length: {len(transitions)}")

# Look at the first few transitions to see initial state
if len(history) > 0:
    first_frame = history[0].frame
    seg = first_frame.segmentation
    print("\nFirst frame objects (N and Y):")
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60:
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Look at the last few transitions
print("\nLast 5 transitions:")
for i, t in enumerate(transitions[-5:]):
    print(f"  Transition {len(transitions)-5+i}: action={t.action}")
    # Check if board changed
    if hasattr(t, 'before_frame') and hasattr(t, 'after_frame'):
        before_seg = t.before_frame.segmentation
        after_seg = t.after_frame.segmentation
        # Compare N and Y objects
        before_ny = [(n['color'], n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60]
        after_ny = [(n['color'], n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60]
        if before_ny != after_ny:
            print(f"    N/Y objects changed!")
            print(f"    Before: {before_ny}")
            print(f"    After: {after_ny}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find G objects
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'G' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60:
        print(f"G object: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
        center_row = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  Center: ({center_row}, {center_col})")

# Try clicking on the green G indicator (id=1)
# boundary=[[9, 27], [11, 27]] - vertical bar at col 27, rows 9-11
# Click at (10, 27)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 27}])
print(f"\nAfter clicking green G: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check if anything changed
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nG objects after click:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'G' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 60:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
